_meta.description: Phase 4 v5 testnet deploy artifacts (plan-008 U18/U19 + D-037). v5 is a fresh breaking republish of v4: Model3D gained `glb_blob_id: String` (mirrors lineage_blob_id — same MAX_BLOB_ID_LEN bound + EBlobIdMalformed code + accessor), and new_model/publish gained a `glb_blob_id` param. Adding a field to an existing `key` struct is not in-place upgradeable → new original-id. The GLB is now uploaded as a STANDALONE Walrus blob (not quilted) and resolved via /v1/blobs/<glb_blob_id>, closing the L1 GLB-resolution gap (Browse previews the base mesh; U12 forks a base). Everything from v4 carries over unchanged: Model3D is a shared object; the only TransferPolicy is for the L2 NftToken and it carries ONLY the royalty rule (D-036 removed kiosk_lock_rule + personal_kiosk_rule) so a bought NftToken is freely usable; mint_nft_token public_transfers a PLAIN OWNED token (no auto-Kiosk); listing-for-sale is a separate opt-in Kiosk PTB. The generic `transfer_policy_id` / `transfer_policy_cap_id` fields hold the NftToken policy. Field names kept generic for frontend-config stability (networkConfig.ts parity). Per D-009: testnet is the 6/21 submission target; mainnet ceremony deferred to the 8/27 prize window.
_meta.package_version: v5 (Phase 4, D-037 — Model3D += glb_blob_id; standalone-blob GLB resolution)
_meta.transfer_policy_rules: royalty_rule ONLY (D-036 removed kiosk_lock_rule + personal_kiosk_rule). Verified at the v5 bootstrap: the deployed TransferPolicy<NftToken> rules VecSet contents = [ 0xe308bb3e…::royalty_rule::Rule ] (exactly one rule); the ensure_collection_policy tx created exactly one RuleKey dynamic field (royalty_rule::Rule under 0xe308bb3e…).
_meta.kiosk_apps_package_id_discovery: Unchanged from v2/v3/v4 (deps pinned identically — apps@7a07937). The deployed TransferPolicy<NftToken> royalty rule TypeName lives under 0xe308bb3e…. Frontend MUST use the address pinned here, NOT the @mysten/kiosk SDK defaults — otherwise confirm_request fails the rule membership check.
